title: Proclamation No. 2396, s. 1985
text:
MALACAÑAN PALACE MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 2396

RELEASING FROM THE NURSERY SITE OF THE BUREAU OF PLANT INDUSTRY A CERTAIN PORTION OF THE LAND EMBRACED THEREIN SITUATED IN BAGO OSHIRO, MINTAL, CITY OF DAVAO AND DECLARING THE SAME FOR USE OF THE INTEGRATED NATIONAL POLICE, IN ACCORDANCE WITH EXISTING LAWS.

WHEREAS, since 1972, the National Police Commission, through its Regional Police Training Center in Bago, Oshiro, Mintal, Davao City has been conducting a continuous and progressive training program for the local police and other allied services on that region;

WHEREAS, upon the constitution of the Integrated National Police in 1975, this function and training facility was transferred to the Integrated National Police;

WHEREAS, since its establishment in 1972 up to the present, this regional police training center has occupied a tract of land belonging to the ministry of Agriculture and Natural Resources;

WHEREAS, in order to foster better services and standards in the training of police personnel in that area, it is imperative that the present site occupied by the Regional Training Center of the Integrated National Police in Bago Oshiro, Mintal, Davao City, be placed under the exclusive jurisdiction and control of the Integrated National Police.

NOW, THEREFORE, I, FERDINAND E. MARCOS, President of the Philippines, by virtue of the power vested in me by law, and upon recommendation of the Minister of Agriculture and Natural Resources, do hereby release from the nursery site of the Bureau of Plant Industry secured by virtue of the provisions of Executive Order No. 372, series of 1950, a certain portion of land, situated in Bago Oshiro Mintal, City of Davao and to reserve the same for the use of the Integrated National Police, in accordance with existing laws, which portion of land is more particularly described, to wit:

Lot A-Psd-11-002533

A PARCEL OF LAND Lot A, Psd-11-002533 being a portion of PC 1221 situated in the Barrio of Mintal, City of Davao, Island of Mindanao, Bounded as follows: On the N., along line 4-5 by lot C of the subdivision plan; on the NE., along line 5-1 by lot C of the subdivision plan; on the SE., along line 1-2 by lot C of the subdivision plan; on the W., along line 2-3-4 by lot C of the subdivision plan; Beginning at a point marked “I” on plan being S. 38 deg. 54’e., 965.53 m. from MBM No. 16, Cad-102, Davao Cadastre.

thence S. 56 deg. 34’W., 254.58 m. to point 2; thence N. 44 deg. 40’W., 2.40 m. to point 3; thence N. 2 deg. 10’E., 136.03 m. to point 4; thence N. 87 deg. 26’E., 205.08 m. to point 5; thence S. 32 deg. 03’E., 7.76 m. to point of

beginning. Containing an area of FIFTEEN THOUSAND ONE HUNDRED NINETY (15,190) SQUARE METERS, more or less. All points referred to are indicated on the plan and are marked on the ground as follows: Points, all point are marked P.S. cyl. con. mon 15×60 cms. Bearings True. Date or original survey, July 14-16, 1977, by Geodetic Engineer GERONIMO S. PALERMO:

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 8th day of February, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and eighty-five.

(Sgd.) FERDINAND E. MARCOS President of the Philippines

BY THE PRESIDENT: (Sgd.) JUAN C. TUVERA Presidential Executive Assistant

Source: CDAsia
